## Cases mentioned in passing

### National Lab stainless steel welding in glove boxes
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "National Lab stainless steel welding in glove boxes"
- **Anchor:** `§6.p7`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "It's very important with stainless steels that you weld in an inert environment, because at high temperatures the chromium oxides form very rapidly, and you get what's called sugaring. You get this black oxide all over everything. That can be done in a variety of ways. It can be done with shielding gas, where you blow gas through the welding torch. A lot of times you have to set up some kind of a backing gas system as well, where you're blowing gas on the backside of it. I used to work for a national lab where we welded stainless steel parts in glove boxes, because we wanted to be very..."
